checks.detail: Each test requires byte-exact diff against GNU ls output; mismatches and build failures score 0.
Reward is solely output equivalence to GNU ls, so any Rust implementation that matches bytes scores equally regardless of internal algorithm.

Sub-dimensions (color, ignore/hide, -Z context, flag combos) are all flattened into one tier as equally weighted binary test cases, so no single dimension is trivially maxable nor unreasonably hard relative to the others.
Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail byte-exact diff against GNU ls; build-from-source enforcement plus oracle-binary hash check block trivial cheats.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace, reads only a subscores file, and never imports agent code so it can be re-run independently.
All paths (success, --fail, --fail --cheat) write score (float), subscores (list, [] on failure), and additional_data via atomic tmpfile+os.replace.

reward_design.formula: score = sum_over_tiers( (tier_pass / tier_total) * WEIGHTS[tier] ), clamped to [0,1]; only tier8_color_advanced is in scope with weight 1.0, so score = tier8_pass / tier8_total
reward_design.formula_explanation: Pass-rate within the single in-scope tier, where each test is a binary byte-exact diff against GNU ls reference output.

components.name: tier8_color_advanced
components.metric: fraction of tier8 test cases whose normalized stdout matches GNU ls byte-for-byte
components.gate: build-from-source required, banned-crate/anti-cheat static greps, strace tripwire on reward writes or execve of ls/dir/vdir/oracle, sha256 not equal to oracle

dimension_analysis.name: color_output
dimension_analysis.notes: Requires byte-exact LS_COLORS escape sequences (di/ln/ex/or/su/sg/tw/ow/st, extension fallbacks).

dimension_analysis.name: selinux_context_Z
dimension_analysis.notes: Print '?' column in the right position; easy in isolation but combined with -l.
